data.title: Autodesk Is Maintained at Overweight by Piper Sandler
data.description: (09:18 GMT) Autodesk Price Target Raised to $373.00/Share From $361.00 by Piper Sandler

data.title: Malaysia's Petronas posts lower H1 profit, revenue
data.description:
Malaysian state energy firm Petroliam Nasional Bhd on Friday reported a drop in first half profit for 2025 compared with the same period last year, citing impacts from some discontinued operations, unfavourable foreign exchange moves and lower average realised prices from its petroleum products.

Petronas posted a profit after tax of 26.2 billion ringgit ($6.2 billion) in the six months ending June 30, down from 32.4 billion ringgit in the corresponding period last year, it said in a statement on Friday.

Revenue for the first half declined to 132.6 billion ringgit, compared with 173.6 billion ringgit a year earlier.

($1 = 4.2050 ringgit)

data.title: CK Hutchison weighs potential listing of global telecom business, Bloomberg News reports
data.description: Conglomerate CK Hutchison is mulling options including a possible listing of its global telecom business, Bloomberg News reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

data.title: Italy August EU-harmonised CPI stable at 1.7% y/y, 'core' inflation accelerates
data.description:
Italian EU-harmonised consumer prices (HICP) fell by a preliminary 0.2% month-on-month in August, with annual inflation stable at 1.7%, data showed on Friday.

The reading was slightly below a median forecast in a Reuters survey of 17 analysts, which pointed to a decrease of 0.1% month-on-month and an year-on-year rise of 1.8%.

Official statistics agency ISTAT also reported that the main domestic price index (NIC), was up 0.1% on the month and up 1.6% annually in August, following a 1.7% annual rate in July.

Core inflation (net of fresh food and energy) was running at 2.2% year-on-year on the HICP index in August, up from 2.1% the month before.

data.title: REG - VietNam Holding Ltd - Estimated Daily Net Asset Value
data.description:
RNS Number : 2308X VietNam Holding Limited 29 August 2025  

For immediate release

VietNam Holding Limited ("VNH" or the "Company")

Estimated Daily Net Asset Value (NAV)

Please note that on Monday, 01 Sep 2025, and Tuesday, 02 Sep 2025, no Daily NAV announcement will be made, as Vietnam's stock markets will be closed for Vietnam National Day.

The Company announces that, as of the close of business on 29 Aug 2025, its Estimated NAV was USD 141.0 million or USD 6.006 per share and GBP 104.6 million or GBP 4.455 per share (GBP/USD = 1.3482).

Change in position as of 29 Aug 2025:

NAV per share (USD)

Vietnam All Share Index (USD)

Daily performance is calculated by reference to the previous day NAV/share and VNAS (Vietnam All Share Index). Calendar month-to-date performance is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 31 Jul 2025. Calendar year-to-date performance is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS at the previous calendar year end (31 Dec 2024). 1-year performance is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 29 Aug 2024. 3-year CAGR (compound annual growth rate) is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 29 Aug 2022. 5-year CAGR is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 29 Aug 2020. 10-year CAGR is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 29 Aug 2015. 15-year CAGR is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 29 Aug 2010. Since inception CAGR is calculated by reference to NAV/share as of 30 Jun 2006. VNAS is sourced from Bloomberg, which uses back-tested positions for VNAS for before 27 Jan 2014 as released by HoSE (Ho Chi Minh City Stock Exchange).

The Estimated Daily NAV stated in this announcement is based on estimated valuations and accordingly the Company accepts no responsibility for the accuracy of the Estimated Daily NAV figure given, and neither is any responsibility implied. The actual month end NAV in any month may vary from the Estimated Daily NAV provided in the relevant month. Accordingly, no reliance should be placed on the Estimated Daily NAV, and it has been provided for indicative purposes only.

More information on the Company is available at www.vietnamholding.com

data.title: Italian Inflation Rate Inches Lower
data.description:
The annual consumer price inflation in Italy inched lower to 1.6% in August of 2025 from 1.7% in the previous month, below market expectations of 1.7% to mark the softest increase since May, according to a preliminary estimate.

In the meantime, the EU-harmonized rate also inched lower by 0.1 percentage point to 1.7%, contributing softer inflationary pressures in the Eurozone.

Inflation eased for regulated energy goods (12.9% vs 17.1% in July) and communication services (0.2% vs 0.5%), while consumer prices fell further for non-regulated energy goods (-5.9% vs -5.2%).

On the other hand, prices accelerated for non-processed (5.6% vs 5.1%) and processed (3% vs 2.8%) food and cultural and recreational services (2.9% vs 2.7%).

Core inflation, which excludes fresh food and energy, inched higher to 2.1% vs 2% in July.

From the previous month, the Italian CPI rose by 0.1%.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 5 AM ET: Global Markets Mixed as Focus Shifts to Inflation Data | Trump ...
data.description:
Global Markets Mixed as Focus Shifts to Inflation Data

Focus now turns to the release of the PCE index, the Fed's preferred measure of inflation.

----

Trump Leans on National Security to Justify Next Wave of Tariffs

The administration plans to expand a certain category of tariffs in hopes of redirecting production to the U.S. and thwarting potential legal threats in the trade war.

----

Fed's Waller backs a series of interest-rate cuts over the next six months

Federal Reserve governor Christopher Waller on Thursday said he now supports a series of interest-rate cuts beginning in September, with the pace of subsequent moves driven by the incoming data.

----

Inflation Shows No Sign of Accelerating in France, Spain as ECB Mulls Next Steps

The results, from two of the eurozone's largest economies, support the slim possibility of a fresh cut to interest rates next month.

----

Higher Prices Are Coming for Household Staples

Companies from Hormel to Ace Hardware are forecasting rising prices as the costs of President Trump's tariffs are passed on to consumers.

----

Fed Governor Lisa Cook Sues to Stop Trump From Firing Her

The lawsuit tees up a high-stakes court battle over the president's authority to remove members of the central bank's board.

----

U.S. Economy Grew Faster in Second Quarter, New Estimate Shows

Consumer spending and AI investment add to the revision in quarterly GDP.

----

New Tariffs on Low-Cost Imports Could Bring in $10 Billion a Year

Some countries have temporarily halted the so-called de minimis shipments in anticipation of the rule change.

----

Trump's push for lower interest rates is on a collision course with government bonds

Bond investors are taking a "wait-and-see" approach to President Donald Trump's attempt to fire Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook.

----

E.U. to Scrap Duties on U.S. Industrial Goods as It Scrambles to Soften Auto Tariff Blow

The European Union will move to eliminate all tariffs on U.S. industrial imports and expand access for American farm products, part of an effort to shield European automakers from steeper duties on their exports to the U.S.

----

Oil Glut Concerns Cloud Price Outlook Despite Geopolitical Risks

A survey compiled by WSJ showed Brent crude is expected to average $63.57 a barrel in the fourth quarter, while West Texas Intermediate is seen at $60.30 a barrel. That is down from last month's projections of $64.13 and $61.11, respectively.

----

Jobless-claims retreat is a sign that recent increase was not the start of a trend

First-time claims fall 5,000 to 229,000 in latest week as continuing claims also moderate.

----

Europe Moves to Reimpose Sanctions on Iran for Nuclear Work

The move by the U.K., France and Germany aims to pressure Iran back into nuclear talks with the U.S.

----

Eurozone's Economic Outlook Worsens Amid U.S. Tariffs, Domestic Pressures

Confidence in the eurozone's economic outlook fell back as sluggish growth weighed on sentiment, with little hope of a major rebound ahead.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Company Headlines at 5 AM ET: Alibaba Creates AI Chip to Help China Fill Nvidia Void | Cambricon ...
data.description:
Alibaba Creates AI Chip to Help China Fill Nvidia Void

Chinese chip companies and artificial-intelligence developers are building up their arsenal of homegrown technology, backed by a government determined to win the AI race.

----

Cambricon Technologies Warns of Trading Risks After Stock Surges

The chip maker overtook liquor maker Kweichow Moutai as China's most valuable stock this week.

----

Caterpillar Says Higher Tariff Costs May Reach $1.8 Billion

The construction and mining manufacturer is now expecting tariffs to have a greater impact on its finances.

----

Gap Expects Stronger Headwinds From Tariffs This Fiscal Year

The apparel company forecast larger ripples from tariffs this fiscal year given recent changes in trade policy, but said it expects to mitigate any added costs over time.

----

Marvell's stock is falling as its earnings didn't live up to the AI hype

The chip company said its record revenue of $2 billion was driven by AI demand for its custom chips, but Marvell didn't deliver upside on its top line.

----

Petco Boosts Earnings Outlook, Swings to Profit

The pet-products retailer raised its full-year earnings outlook after swinging to a profit in its latest quarter as its turnaround progresses.

----

Ulta Beauty Lifts Fiscal-Year Guidance as Second-Quarter Profit, Sales Rise

The cosmetics and fragrances retailer boosted its guidance for the fiscal year.

----

Autodesk Bumps Up Guidance as Sales Jump

The company, which provides AutoCAD and other design software, logged double-digit sales growth in its latest quarter, helped by strong demand for its architecture, engineering, construction and operations software.

----

Affirm Swings to Profit as Top Merchants Boost Volume

The buy now, pay later company saw gross merchandise volume at its top five merchants and platform partners increasing 41%.

----

With Outcome Uncertain, UAW Claims Battery Plant Win

A contest over United Auto Workers union representation at a Kentucky EV battery plant of Ford Motor joint-venture BlueOval SK is coming come down to a handful of disputed ballots.

----

Hormel to Raise Prices, Citing Costlier Pork, Beef and Nuts

The Spam maker is the latest company to warn of higher prices for food and household goods. Rising commodity costs led it to reduce its profit outlook.

----

Dick's Sporting Goods Lifts Outlook as Sales Climb

The retailer raised its full-year guidance after a strong first half as it comes within weeks of acquiring Foot Locker, though a softening of its gross margin outlook weighed on shares.

----

Lawmakers Press UnitedHealth on Hack Loan Repayments

The senators probe tactics the healthcare giant used to collect debts after a 2024 cyberattack.

----

Stada Eyes IPO in Autumn if Conditions Are Right

The German pharmaceutical company is backed by private-equity firms Bain Capital and Cinven.

data.title: Croatia Retail Sales Growth Slows Sharply
data.description:
Retail sales in Croatia rose 1.4% year-on-year in July 2025, easing from a 7.5% jump in the previous month.

This marked the slowest increase since a decline in March 2023, as growth moderated for non-food products (7.6% vs 11.5% in June), automotive fuels and lubricants (3% vs 3.4%), and internet or mail-order trade (4.1% vs 5.5%).

At the same time, sales of food, beverages, and tobacco fell 1.9%, after a 3.6% rise in June.

On a monthly basis, retail sales contracted by 4.0%, the steepest drop since April 2020, reversing a 3.5% increase in June, as sales declined for all components.

For the January–July period, retail sales grew 3.8% compared to the same period a year earlier.

data.title: Greek Producer Prices Slip in July
data.description:
The annual producer prices in Greece slipped to 0.5% in July 2025 from a 1.6% rise in the previous month.

This marked the first producer deflation since December 2024, as prices tumbled for energy (-1% vs 2.9% in June).

In addition, prices moderated for capital goods (2% vs 3.1%) and remained unchanged for non-durable consumer goods (0.6%).

On the other hand, prices increased at a faster pace for intermediate goods (2.3% vs 2%) and durable consumer goods (5.7% vs 4.7%).

On a monthly basis, producer prices rose by 0.2%, slowing from a 1.1% gain in the preceding month.

data.title: Bosnia's economy to grow 2.4% in 2025, downside risks elevated, IMF says
data.description:
The International Monetary Fund expects Bosnia and Herzegovina's economy to grow 2.4% in 2025 and 3% in 2026, driven by consumption and exports but with elevated downside risks, it said in a staff report on Friday.

"Trade uncertainty, a slowdown in Europe, volatile commodity prices, tighter global financial conditions and further escalation of political tensions pose significant downside risks to growth," the IMF Executive Board said after completing an Article IV consultation for the Balkan country.

Bosnia's economy grew 2.5% last year.

The IMF underscored the importance of Bosnia maintaining political stability, avoiding policy slippages and leveraging the EU accession process to accelerate reforms.

Bosnia's reform process has been blocked over the past year due to a political crisis triggered by the Bosnian Serb separatist drive, widely seen as one of the biggest threats to peace in the Balkans since the 1995 Dayton peace accords that ended the fighting in Bosnia.

Inflation is seen at 3.8% in 2025 due to higher imported food prices, more than doubling from 1.7% in the previous year, the Washington-based lender said. It warned that strong growth in real wages and private sector credit could put upward pressure on inflation, which stood at 2.3% in May, the latest available data.

The current account deficit is expected to widen to 4.1% of GDP from 4% in 2024, with a recovery in electricity exports after last year's droughts and lower oil prices offsetting adverse effects of U.S. tariffs on exports.

IMF directors highlighted the importance of safeguarding central bank independence and further strengthening the bank's policy toolkit to preserve the credibility of the currency board arrangement, which remains an anchor of stability.
